evals.prompt: I have an empty project directory and want to start using linked-intent development on it. Please set it up. Use Full LID.

assertions.text: docs/llds/ directory exists in the project
docs/specs/ directory exists in the project
docs/high-level-design.md exists, populated from the HLD template (not empty)
CLAUDE.md contains the literal string '## LID Mode: Full' as a heading
CLAUDE.md contains the LID directives block referencing 'linked-intent-dev'
The agent's response summarizes what files were created or modified

evals.prompt: I already have a CLAUDE.md with my team's existing conventions. I want to adopt linked-intent development without losing any of my existing content. Set it up.

files.content:
# My Project

## Conventions

- Prefer functional style where possible.
- Use TypeScript strict mode.
- Don't add dependencies without discussion.

assertions.text: CLAUDE.md still contains all of the original content ('# My Project', '## Conventions', and the three bullet points) unmodified
CLAUDE.md has the LID directives block appended (contains 'linked-intent-dev' or 'Linked-Intent Development')
CLAUDE.md contains the '## LID Mode:' heading
The agent's response explicitly summarizes which sections were added to CLAUDE.md

evals.prompt: Please set up LID in this project.

files.content:
# My Project

## LID Mode: Full

## Linked-Intent Development (MANDATORY)

**Consult the `linked-intent-dev` skill for ALL code changes.** All changes flow through the arrow of intent in one direction:

```
HLD → LLDs → EARS → Tests → Code
```

assertions.text: No file changes were made (CLAUDE.md and existing docs are unmodified)
The agent's response informs the user what was detected (mode, directory status) rather than silently doing nothing
